descriptions: Strisciuglio clan is listed as a rival of Misceo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Misceo clan

descriptions: Strisciuglio clan violently overtook the Misceo clan in San Paolo.
edges.evidence_quote: The clan's tentacles have thus enveloped the San Paolo neighbourhood, where they have violently gripped the Misceo clan

descriptions: Kodo-kai succeeded Hirota-gumi after its disbandment.
edges.evidence_quote: Shinobu Tsukasa formed the Kodo-kai as the successor to the Hirota-gumi

descriptions: Sasaki-gumi is a sub-organization of Hirota-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sasaki-gumi was a sub organization of the Nagoya-based Hirota-gumi

descriptions: Hirota-gumi is an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: He began his yakuza career in 1962 when he joined the Hirota-gumi, a Nagoya -based Yamaguchi-gumi affiliate.

descriptions: Kodo-kai is the successor to Hirota-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the disbanding of the Hirota-gumi, he founded the Kodo-kai with Kiyoshi Takayama among others in 1984 as the successor to the Hirota-gumi.

descriptions: Hirota-gumi is a subsidiary family within Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: wakagashira-hosa Shinobu Tsukasa (then kumichō of the Hirota-gumi) was chosen as new wakagashira

Sakaume-gumi.description: The Sakaume-gumi is a yakuza organization based in Osaka, Japan, founded by Umekichi Tobi. It is a small, old-established group focusing primarily on gambling, with relatively good relations with local police. It was registered as a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law in May 1993.

descriptions: The De Bernardo clan is a satellite clan that answers to the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Somma Vesuviana: vi è un clan satellite che fa capo ai Mazzarella, il clan De Bernardo.

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Dragone è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese originaria di Cutro alleata con i Mannolo di San Leonardo di Cutro e gli Arena di Isola Capo Rizzuto e gli Arabia di Cutro.

descriptions: Guerrisi and Mercuri united through marriage in the 1920s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni venti, i Guerrisi s'unirono con i Mercuri

descriptions: Infobox lists Rusich as an affiliate, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: Pyatnashka Brigade, Interbrigades, Rusich, Espanyola

descriptions: Rusich is a sub-group of Wagner Group.
edges.evidence_quote: A Wagner sub-group, "Rusich", was founded by self-proclaimed neo-Nazi Alexey Milchakov and is open about its far-right ideology.

Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Pennsylvania).description: American outlaw motorcycle club formed in Philadelphia in 1967. The club is involved in narcotics distribution, prostitution, kidnapping, burglary, sale of stolen property, violent crime, and murder. It is most prominent in the Delaware Valley and has chapters in several Mid-Atlantic states. Membership is restricted to white males over 18 who own a Harley-Davidson motorcycle.

descriptions: The Breed is a rival gang to the Warlocks.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival gangs include the Breed and the Florida Warlocks.

descriptions: The Warlocks are allied with the Pagans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Warlocks are allied with the Pagans and have associated in criminal activities with the Philadelphia crime family.

descriptions: The Warlocks have associated in criminal activities with the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Warlocks are allied with the Pagans and have associated in criminal activities with the Philadelphia crime family.

descriptions: The Florida Warlocks are a rival gang to the Warlocks.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival gangs include the Breed and the Florida Warlocks.

descriptions: Alkhalil family members were tried for murdering members of the rival Dhak-Duhre gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Starting on 29 June 2021, Amero and Alkhalil were on trial in Vancouver for the 2012 murders of the rival gangsters Sandip Duhre and Suhhveer Dhak

descriptions: The Alkhalil family feud with the Dhak-Duhre group started after Khalil's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Naud was working as a drug dealer for the Dhak-Duhre group, and the killing of Khalil started a feud.

descriptions: Dhak-Duhre gang was allied with the United Nations gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dhak-Duhre group was allied with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: Infobox lists Dhak crime group as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Dhak crime group

descriptions: Independent Soldiers were involved in a war with Dhak-Duhre group after Dhak's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Gurmit Singh Dhak, the co-boss of the Dhak-Duhre group was murdered... Dhak's execution was the flashpoint for a near decade-long war

descriptions: The Dhak-Duhre group killed Independent Soldier members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Two hitmen from the Dhak-Duhre organization were able to enter...

descriptions: The Dhak-Duhre gang was allied with the United Nations gang against the Red Scorpions.
edges.evidence_quote: Allied with the United Nations gang against the Red Scorpions was the Dhak-Duhre gang.

descriptions: Wolfpack Alliance was believed responsible for murder of Gurmit Dhak, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Sukhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack gang and that Amero, Riach, and Jonathan Bacon in particular were the ones responsible for his brother's murder.

descriptions: Wolfpack was believed responsible for murder of a Dhak-Duhre member, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Suhhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack gang and that Amero and Bacon in particular were the ones responsible for his brother's murder.

descriptions: Wolfpack was in a violent conflict with the Dhak-Duhre group, with murders on both sides.
edges.evidence_quote: Suhhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack Alliance were the ones responsible for his brother's murder, and vowed to avenge him.

descriptions: SSIA split from Noom Suk Harn.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1960, the movement suffered an internal split between the 'rough and uneducated' Saw Yanda and a group of young intellectuals, and the latter subsequently formed the Shan State Independence Army (SSIA).

descriptions: SSIA and Kokang Force merged to form SSA.
edges.evidence_quote: The SSIA, the SNUF, and the Kokang Force agreed to merge into the Shan State Army (SSA) in 1964.

descriptions: SSIA and SNUF merged to form SSA.
edges.evidence_quote: The SSIA, the SNUF, and the Kokang Force agreed to merge into the Shan State Army (SSA) in 1964.

descriptions: The Little Gents was a constituent gang that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.

descriptions: Infobox lists Espanyola as an affiliate, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: Pyatnashka Brigade, Interbrigades, Rusich, Espanyola

descriptions: Kuratong Baleleng had ties to Maguindanao guerrillas, leading to Muslim clans joining.
edges.evidence_quote: ties to the Maguindanao guerrillas led Muslim Maguindanaon clans to join

descriptions: The two factions are in conflict over the name.
edges.evidence_quote: Han ocurrido muchos asesinatos debido a que ambas organizaciones reclaman el título de 'Mafia Mexicana' en el sistema penitenciario de Arizona.

descriptions: La Nueva Mafia Mexicana is a splinter group from the original Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: En 1978, La eMe se dividió en dos organizaciones: una que conservaba la estructura y filosofía original, La Mafia Mexicana Original, y otra, La Nueva Mafia Mexicana, con su propia estructura

descriptions: Montescuro mediated between Missos, Mazzarellas and Continis during a war.
edges.evidence_quote: he was able to put the Missos, the Mazzarellas and the Continis at the same table when the war between their organisations was at its peak in the early 2000s

descriptions: The Vallelunga and Turrà were opposed to the Ciconte and their allies in the Faida dei boschi.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Turrà, contrapporsi allo schieramento formato dagli Emanuele di Mongiana (intesi strazzi), agli Emanuele di Santa Caterina, ai Ciconte di Serra San Bruno e ai Nardo di Soriano

descriptions: Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi are linked to Molè as part of the dominant group.
edges.evidence_quote: legate al gruppo dominante sull’intera piana di Gioia Tauro delle famiglie mafiose dei Piromalli e dei Molè

descriptions: Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi are linked to Piromalli as part of the dominant group.
edges.evidence_quote: legate al gruppo dominante sull’intera piana di Gioia Tauro delle famiglie mafiose dei Piromalli e dei Molè

descriptions: Viola and Zagari were in a violent feud with Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 luglio 1989 con l'uccisione di Rocco Neri scoppia una violenta faida tra gli Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi e gli Zagari-Viola.

descriptions: Zagari and Viola were in a violent feud with Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 luglio 1989 con l'uccisione di Rocco Neri scoppia una violenta faida tra gli Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi e gli Zagari-Viola.

descriptions: Cepparulo was a member of Barbudos, killed by De Luca Bossa associates.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Cepparulo, ritenuto esponente del cosiddetto clan dei Barbudos

descriptions: Morbids aligned with Bandidos, drawing them into conflict with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: the Morbids Motorcycle Club, a small club with only six members, refused to capitulate to the Hells Angels and instead aligned themselves with the Bandidos.

descriptions: Morbids MC patched over to become Bandidos chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos were established in Sweden after patching over the Morbids Motorcycle Club in Helsingborg as a probationary chapter in January 1994.

descriptions: Morbids aligned with Bandidos during Nordic Biker War.
edges.evidence_quote: the Morbids aligned with the Bandidos

descriptions: Morbids were a Hells Angels hangaround club.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morbids had previously acted as a Hells Angels hangaround club and vied with other clubs for the right to become the Angels' first Swedish chapter

descriptions: Morbids and Rebels were rival clubs that clashed.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morbids were later offered a path to Hells Angels membership on the condition that they merge with the Rebels, a rival club with whom they had previously clashed.

descriptions: Jackals are a Hells Angels puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: four members of the Jackals, a Hells Angels puppet gang in London, Ontario, were involved in a shoot-out at the house of an Outlaw

descriptions: IS lists Liwa Abu al-Fadhal al-Abbas as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: ... Liwa Abu al-Fadhal al-Abbas

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Los Tangueros became ACCU in 1994.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1994, Carlos took control of Los Tangueros, which officially changed their name to the Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Uraba (ACCU).

'Ndrina D'Agostino.description: The D'Agostino are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, with branches in Rosarno, Laureana di Borrello, Sant'Ilario dello Ionio, and Canolo. They are historically powerful, acting as a link between Reggio and Locri clans and representing the 'Ndrangheta within Cosa Nostra in the 1970s. They have been involved in international drug trafficking and feuds, such as the Faida di Laureana and Faida di Sant'Ilario. Many members are currently imprisoned, but the organization remains active.

descriptions: Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone clashed with Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Belcastro-Romeo and D'Agostino were in conflict in the Sant'Ilario feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Sant'Ilario : ... si apre una nuova faida con i Belcastro-Romeo che vogliono distaccarsi dai D'Agostino.

descriptions: Belcastro-Romeo faction is in conflict with D'Agostino.
edges.evidence_quote: si apre una nuova faida tra i Belcastro-Romeo (Giuseppe Belcastro e Tommaso Romeo) che vogliono distaccarsi dai D'Agostino

descriptions: Belcastro-Romeo fought against D'Agostino in the Faida di Sant'Ilario.
edges.evidence_quote: si apre una nuova faida tra i Belcastro-Romeo (Giuseppe Belcastro e Tommaso Romeo) si vogliono distaccare dai D'Agostino

descriptions: D'Agostino committed a murder on commission from Bellocco.
edges.evidence_quote: al capobastone Giuseppe D'Agostino sono stati imputati ben 10 omicidi, compresa l'uccisione su commissione della cosca Bellocco

descriptions: D'Agostino are allies of Bellocco.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei De Stefano e dei Bellocco

descriptions: D'Agostino are allies of De Stefano.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei De Stefano e dei Bellocco

descriptions: D'Agostino operate together with Raso-Gullace-Albanese in Canolo.
edges.evidence_quote: operano nel locale di Canolo insieme ai Raso-Gullace-Albanese

descriptions: D'Agostino were representatives of 'Ndrangheta within Cosa Nostra in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: ha indicato i D'Agostino come i rappresentanti della 'ndrangheta in seno a Cosa Nostra negli anni' 70

descriptions: D'Agostino forms part of the Bellocco-D'Agostino-Cacciola cosca.
edges.evidence_quote: Il casato che opera nella locale di Rosarno formando la cosca Bellocco-D'Agostino-Cacciola imparentati tra di loro

descriptions: D'Agostino acted as a link between Serraino-Condello-Imerti and Commisso-Cordì-Aquino.
edges.evidence_quote: l'anello di congiunzione tra le cosche reggine facenti capo ai Serraino-Condello-Imerti e quelle della Locride (Commisso-Cordì-Aquino)

descriptions: The locale of Canolo, which D'Agostino are part of, is part of the Corona superstructure.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2013 con l'operazione Saggezza, si scopre che il locale di Canolo di cui fanno parte a sua volta fa parte di una sovrastruttura chiamata "Corona".

descriptions: D'Agostino are part of the group leading the Laureana di Borrello locale in Sardinia.
edges.evidence_quote: In Sardegna opera il locale di Laureana di Borrello , con a capo i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino.

descriptions: D'Agostino acted as a link between Serraino-Condello-Imerti and Commisso-Cordì-Aquino.
edges.evidence_quote: l'anello di congiunzione tra le cosche reggine facenti capo ai Serraino-Condello-Imerti e quelle della Locride (Commisso-Cordì-Aquino)

descriptions: Romeo, allied with Belcastro, is in conflict with D'Agostino.
edges.evidence_quote: si apre una nuova faida tra i Belcastro-Romeo (Giuseppe Belcastro e Tommaso Romeo) che vogliono distaccarsi dai D'Agostino

Keane-Collopy gang.description: Criminal organisation based in Limerick City, Ireland, founded in the 1990s by Christy Keane. Major drug suppliers in the Mid-West region, heavily involved in the Limerick feud since 2000. Has been weakened in recent years with key figures dead or imprisoned.

descriptions: The Hutch gang is allied with the Keane-Collopy Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Keane-Collopy Gang , McCarthy-Dundon Gang , RIRA , New IRA

descriptions: Keane-Collopy gang is allied with the Hutch Organised Crime Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hutch Organised Crime Gang

descriptions: Keane-Collopy gang is in conflict with the McCarthy-Dundon gang as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: McCarthy-Dundon Gang

descriptions: Keane-Collopy gang is in conflict with the Ryan Gang as part of the Limerick feud.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which mainly involves the Keane-Collopys, the Ryans and the McCarthy-Dundon gang

descriptions: The McCarthy-Dundon Gang and the Keane-Collopy Gang are both participants in the Limerick feud, which is a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which involves several criminal families, mainly the McCarthy-Dundons, the Ryans and the Keane-Collopy gang from St. Mary's Park, has been ongoing since 2000.

descriptions: The 110ers Gang feuds with the Young Melph Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years the gang have been known to feud with the Young Melph Mafia and the Ride or Die Gang from the 8th Ward of New Orleans.

descriptions: 3-N-G committed violent acts towards Young Melph Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: They are well known for their murderous reputation in the streets and violent acts towards other gangs including the Young Melph Mafia.

descriptions: Young Melph Mafia is listed as a rival of 39ers gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Young Melph Mafia

descriptions: Byrd Gang has a rivalry with Young Melph Mafia according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ghost Gang Goonie Gang, Young Melph Mafia St Bernard Projects

descriptions: Infobox lists CSP-PSD as an opponent of Wagner Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: List: CSP-PSD

'Ndrina Parrello.description: The Parrello 'ndrina is a mafia clan based in Palmi, Reggio Calabria, Italy. It is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and infiltration of public contracts. The clan has been engaged in feuds with rival 'ndrine such as the Gallico. Recent operations have led to arrests for international drug trafficking and corruption.

descriptions: The Bruzzise are allied with the Parrello.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Bruzzise è una cosca della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Palmi alleata con i Parrello .

descriptions: Bruzzise and Parrello are mentioned together as a single group in operation Cosa mia.
edges.evidence_quote: Le 'ndrine coinvolte sono quelle dei Gallico-Morgante-Sgrò-Sciglitano e Bruzzise -Parrello .

descriptions: The Parrello and Condello are allied in the feud against Gallico.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel maggio del 1977 scoppia una faida tra i Gallico-Morgante-Sgrò-Sciglitano e tra i Parrello - Condello

descriptions: The Parrello and Condello were in a feud against the Gallico.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel maggio del 1977 scoppia una faida tra i Gallico-Morgante-Sgrò-Sciglitano e tra i Parrello - Condello

descriptions: A feud (faida) broke out between Parrello and Gallico in 1986 after the murder of Parrello's boss.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1986, a causa dell'omicidio del capobastone Gaetano Parrello, scoppia una faida tra la 'ndrina e i Gallico .

descriptions: Parrello and Piccolo were in conflict in the second Palmi feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida di Palmi , esplosa il 25 settembre 1986 tra i Parrello e i Piccolo con l'omicidio del capocosca Gaetano Parrello

Fatemi Circle.description: A clique of statesmen within the Government of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, involved in corruption scandals in Iran. Known for embezzlement, money laundering, bribery, and fraud. Based in Tehran, founded around 2005. Allegedly led by Mohammad-Reza Rahimi.

Islamic State – Somalia Province.description: Islamic State affiliate operating primarily in the mountainous regions of Puntland, northern Somalia. Formed in late 2015 when pro-IS fighters defected from al-Shabaab and pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. The group controls a small, sparsely populated area and has an estimated 500–700 fighters. It is officially recognized by IS leadership as Somalia Province and is engaged in conflict with al-Shabaab and Puntland security forces.

descriptions: Al-Shabaab lists Islamic State Somalia Province as an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State Somalia Province (Abnaa ul-Calipha)

descriptions: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Sahel Province (ISSP), formerly known as Islamic State in the Greater Sahara (IS-GS), operates mainly in Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso.

descriptions: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS, formed by defectors from Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Somalia Province (ISSP) operates primarily in Puntland in northern Somalia. The group first appeared in 2015 when elements within the Somali Islamist group Al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS.

descriptions: ISS and al-Shabaab are declared enemies, with active conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: ISS is also the declared enemy of al-Shabaab , which considers the Islamic State a significant threat to its own predominance among Jihadist factions in Somalia.

descriptions: ISS evicted al-Shabaab from contested area, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In course of 2020's first half, ISS gradually increased the number of its attacks, while its troops evicted al-Shabaab from the contested area around Dasaan, Mudug region.

descriptions: ISSP formed from defectors of Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: elements within the Somali Islamist group Al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS.

descriptions: ISS cooperates with Somali pirates for smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: In smuggling fighters and supplies across the Gulf of Aden, ISS works closely with Somali pirates, namely Mohamed Garfanje's Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network and another unidentified group that is based in Qandala.

descriptions: ISS cooperates with the Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, it was revealed that the Islamic State – Somalia Province works closely with Somali pirates, namely Mohamed Garfanje's Hobyo - Haradhere Piracy Network and another unidentified group that is based in Qandala.

descriptions: ISS is an affiliate of the Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Somalia Province ( IS–SP or ISS ) or Abnaa ul-Calipha or also just Islamic State of Somalia is an affiliate of the Islamic State

descriptions: ISS is an affiliate of the Islamic State, pledging allegiance to its caliph.
edges.evidence_quote: The group first appeared in the latter half of 2015 when pro-Islamic State fighters within al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS caliph Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi.

descriptions: ISS sends monthly cryptocurrency payments to ISKP.
edges.evidence_quote: In early 2023, a United Nations (UN) report found that the al-Karrar office in Somalia was sending $ 25,000 per month in cryptocurrency to the Islamic State Khorasan Province

descriptions: IS-GS was formally part of ISWAP from 2019 to 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: From March 2019 to 2022, IS-GS was formally part of the Islamic State – West Africa Province (ISWAP); when it was also called "ISWAP-Greater Sahara".

descriptions: ISS is allied with IS-YP according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: IS-YP , Somali pirates

descriptions: IS-Yemen provides direct support to ISS.
edges.evidence_quote: The group is also directly supported by the Islamic State – Yemen Province, which is known to have sent experts, trainers, money, weapons and other materials to ISS.

Lucchese crime family.description: The Lucchese crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City. Originating in the early 1920s, it was led by Tommy Gagliano and later Tommy Lucchese, becoming a powerful force in organized crime. The family controls rackets including gambling, loansharking, drug trafficking, and labor racketeering, primarily in New York and New Jersey. It is still active today.

descriptions: The Lucchese crime family is listed as an ally of the 116th Street Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Lucchese , Purple Gang

descriptions: Nig Rosen, head of the 69th Street Gang, became partners with the Lucchese crime family through a business venture.
edges.evidence_quote: Rosen became partners with Gaetano "Tommy" Lucchese after buying the Sweet Valley Improvement Company which was used by the Lucchese crime family to ship clothing out of New York's garment district.

descriptions: Lucchese family holds a seat on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Lucchese (1931–present)

descriptions: Bonanno family was in conflict with Lucchese family in the 1960s.
edges.evidence_quote: Joseph Bonanno was now threatened by an alliance of Tommy Lucchese and new boss Carlo Gambino

descriptions: Bonanno mobsters stormed a Lucchese-managed club as a show of force, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2012, he and acting capo John "Johnny Joe" Spirito Jr., as well as 20 to 25 Bonanno mobsters stormed the Lucchese family -managed Coddington Club in the Bronx as a "show of force".

descriptions: A Bonanno soldier was shot by an associate of the Lucchese family, indicating conflict between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: It later became common knowledge that Stagno was shot by Terrance Caldwell, an associate of the Lucchese family.

descriptions: Bonanno planned to assassinate Lucchese boss Tommy Lucchese.
edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses Tommy Lucchese

descriptions: Bonanno family is allied with Lucchese crime family per infobox (later period).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Lucchese crime family

descriptions: A Bonanno soldier attended the wake of a Lucchese consigliere, indicating social ties.
edges.evidence_quote: Armetta was charged with assaulting an individual on Halloween 2015 to gain entry into the Bonanno family, and he was released from prison on February 12, 2020. He attended the wake of former Lucchese family consigliere Frank Lastorino in November 2022.

descriptions: Bonanno member Vincent Asaro was involved in the Lufthansa robbery with Lucchese family associates.
edges.evidence_quote: Asaro was acquitted of alleged involvement in the 1978 Lufthansa robbery with Lucchese family associates

descriptions: Bonanno and Lucchese families met to maintain peace.
edges.evidence_quote: he and Dara met with Lucchese crime family capo Paul Vario and acting boss Ettore Coco in Miami in order to keep the peace between the two crime families.

descriptions: The Lucchese family handled heroin distribution for Spirito's shipments in New York.
edges.evidence_quote: The distribution in New York was handled by the Lucchese Crime Family.

descriptions: Lucchese mobsters threatened Scarpa's grandson, leading to a violent confrontation.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 1992, two Lucchese mobsters, Michael DeRosa and Ronald Moran, threatened Scarpa's grandson Joey over a drug deal.

descriptions: The Lucchese family proposed merging with the Colombo family to end the internal war.
edges.evidence_quote: Lucchese underboss Anthony Casso proposed to merge the family with his own to end the war.

descriptions: The Corleone family's founder was a relative of the founder of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Gagliano, a relative of Tommy Gagliano , who would later establish the Lucchese crime family in New York City .

descriptions: Lucchese is listed as an ally of DeCavalcante in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lucchese crime family

descriptions: DeMeo was initially associated with the Lucchese family before shifting allegiance.
edges.evidence_quote: DeMeo was initially an associate of the Brooklyn faction of the Lucchese crime family

descriptions: Lucchese family underboss Casso ordered DeMeo crew members to kill DeMeo and promised them membership in the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Casso ordered them to kill DeMeo, assuring them that there would be no retribution and that afterwards they would join him in the Lucchese family.

descriptions: The Purple Gang originally affiliated and worked with the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was originally affiliated with and worked with the Lucchese crime family

descriptions: Gallo crew member Vic Amuso transferred to the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Vic Amuso – transferred to Lucchese crime family

descriptions: Lucchese family hitmen murdered Gambino underboss Frank DeCicco.
edges.evidence_quote: 1986 – Frank DeCicco – murdered in 1986 by Lucchese family hitmen.

descriptions: Lucchese underboss ordered murder of Gambino member Bartholomew Boriello.
edges.evidence_quote: He was assassinated in 1991 on orders of Lucchese underboss Anthony Casso.

descriptions: The Gambino family lists Lucchese as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Los Angeles crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, ...

descriptions: Gotti obtained support from the Lucchese family for his coup against Castellano.
edges.evidence_quote: Gotti could also count on the complicity of Gambino consigliere Joseph N. Gallo. ... he got the support of several important figures of his generation in the Lucchese, Colombo and Bonanno families.

descriptions: A Gambino soldier is connected to a Lucchese faction leader, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Merola is connected to Lucchese crime family Jersey faction leader Martin Taccetta.

descriptions: Gambino and Lucchese families were allied from 1953 to 1985, cooperating in Commission plots and rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese-Genovese alliance (1953–1985) between Carlo Gambino, Tommy Lucchese, and Vito Genovese began with a plot to take over the Mafia Commission by murdering family bosses Frank Costello and Albert Anastasia.

descriptions: Renewed alliance between Gambino and Lucchese families from 1999 onward, cooperating in construction extortion and gambling.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese alliance (1999–present) was initiated by acting Luchese boss Steven Crea in 1999.

descriptions: Gambino exerted behind-the-scenes control over the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Gambino's influence also stretched into behind-the-scenes control of the Lucchese crime family, led by Carmine "Mr. Gribbs" Tramunti.

descriptions: Lucchese conspired with Gambino to set up Genovese.
edges.evidence_quote: Gambino, Luciano, Costello, and Lucchese allegedly lured Genovese into a drug-dealing scheme that ultimately resulted in his conspiracy indictment and conviction.

descriptions: Genovese family lists Lucchese as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: Men connected to Genovese and Lucchese families were indicted together for jewelry heists.
edges.evidence_quote: On June 6, 2023, five men with connections to the Genovese and Lucchese crime families were indicted for carrying out two brazen midday Manhattan jewelry heists.

descriptions: Genovese soldier Andrew Gargiulo cooperated with Lucchese family in a sports betting ring.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 1993, Gargiulo was indicted for operating a sports betting ring reputedly worth $86 million annually in cooperation with the Gambino, Colombo and Lucchese families.

descriptions: Genovese soldier Frank DiPietro and associates of the Lucchese family were arrested together for a jewelry theft.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2023, DiPietro and associates of the Lucchese and Gambino families, Michael Sellick, Vincent Spagnuolo, Vincent Cerchio, and Samuel Sore, were arrested for stealing $2 million worth of jewellery

descriptions: Genovese family lists Lucchese as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Detroit, Gambino, Lucchese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia, K&A Gang

descriptions: Valachi moved from Lucchese to Genovese, indicating a transfer of a member between families.
edges.evidence_quote: Valachi was active since the Castellammarese War during the early 1930s, as an associate of the Lucchese crime family; however; after the murder of Salvatore Maranzano in 1931, he joined the Genovese family.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction cooperated with the Lucchese family through a working relationship between Fiumara and Accetturo.
edges.evidence_quote: He also maintained a working relationship with Lucchese Jersey capo Anthony 'Tumac' Accetturo.

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

descriptions: The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of the Reina family, which later became the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the gang stole wallets, burglarized stores, and engaged in other illegal activities. The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of Bronx–East Harlem family boss Gaetano "Tom" Reina .

descriptions: Lucchese family ordered a hit on a Bonanno associate, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 2012, Crea ordered Vincent Bruno and soldier Paul Cassano to murder a Bonanno associate who disrespected him.

descriptions: Lucchese acting boss Madonna attempted to murder a Bonanno soldier.
edges.evidence_quote: Madonna was also accused of the May 2013 attempted murder of Bonanno family soldier Enzo “The Baker” Stagno.

descriptions: Bonanno planned to murder Lucchese, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1963, Joseph Magliocco and Bonanno boss Joseph Bonanno hatched an audacious plan to murder Commission bosses Carlo Gambino , Lucchese, and Stefano Magaddino , as well as Frank DeSimone , and take over the Mafia Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: Cutaia Crew operates in Brooklyn, Queens, and Long Island.

descriptions: Michael Meldish was a former leader of the East Harlem Purple Gang and also a Lucchese hitman, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Michael Meldish, a former leader of the East Harlem Purple Gang and Lucchese Bronx-based hitman.

descriptions: The French Connection Crew was closely aligned with the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: The French Connection Crew (1967–1973) was an organization, closely aligned with the Lucchese family, who were responsible for the theft of approximately $70 million in heroin taken from the NYPD property room.

descriptions: Lucchese family under Amuso and Casso were bitter rivals to John Gotti's Gambino family.
edges.evidence_quote: While they were allies of Genovese family boss Vincent Gigante, they were bitter rivals to Gotti and the Gambinos.

descriptions: Lucchese family used corrupt police to murder Gambino members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Casso used Caracappa and Eppolito to pressure the Gambino crime family by murdering several of their members.

descriptions: Lucchese informant's testimony led to convictions of Gambino members.
edges.evidence_quote: his testimony secured the convictions of more than 60 American Mafia members and associates, including Louis Vallario and Michael DiLeonardo from the Gambino crime family

descriptions: Lucchese and Gambino families cooperated to control organized crime in New York.
edges.evidence_quote: Lucchese partnered with Gambino crime family boss Carlo Gambino to control organized crime in New York, exerting a stronghold over the city's garment industry and other criminal rackets.

descriptions: Marriage between Lucchese's daughter and Gambino's son created a strong alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Lucchese's daughter, Frances would marry Tommy Gambino , the son of Carlo Gambino , the boss of the Gambino crime family , which formed a strong bond between the two crime families.

descriptions: Marriage alliance and exchange of airport rackets solidified cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, Carlo Gambino's oldest son, Thomas Gambino , married Lucchese's daughter Frances. Over 1,000 guests attended the wedding, at which Carlo Gambino presented Lucchese with a $30,000 gift. In return, Lucchese gave Gambino a part of his rackets at Idlewild Airport (since renamed John F. Kennedy Airport ).

descriptions: Joseph DeSena was an associate of the Gambino family before joining the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: DeSena began his criminal career as an associate in the Gambino family before becoming a member of the Lucchese family.

descriptions: Genovese captain ordered murder of a Lucchese associate.
edges.evidence_quote: It is believed his murder was ordered by Genovese crime family captain Anthony Strollo

descriptions: Genovese and Lucchese bosses conspired together to kill Gotti.
edges.evidence_quote: Gigante conspired with Lucchese boss Anthony 'Tony Ducks' Corallo to have Gotti killed.

descriptions: Lucchese family under Amuso and Casso were allies of Genovese family boss Vincent Gigante.
edges.evidence_quote: During the late 1980s, ... they were allies of Genovese family boss Vincent Gigante

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Genovese crime family

descriptions: Lucchese and Genovese families cooperated to target John Gotti.
edges.evidence_quote: Casso, along with the imprisoned Amuso and Genovese crime family boss Vincent Gigante, wanted their rival John Gotti out of the way.

descriptions: Lucchese allied with Genovese and Gambino to take over the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: That same year, Lucchese formed an alliance with Luciano crime family underboss Vito Genovese and Anastasia crime family underboss Carlo Gambino with the long-term goal of gaining control of the Commission.

descriptions: Lucchese family originated from the Morello crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tanglewood Boys was a "recruitment gang" that operated in Westchester County, The Bronx, and Manhattan.

descriptions: The Tanglewood Boys served as a recruitment gang for the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s, Santorelli led the Tanglewood Boys, a recruitment gang for the Lucchese family.

descriptions: Lucchese soldier Petrucelli was also a member of the Tanglewood Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: He had served as a soldier for the Lucchese family, and as a member of the Tanglewood Boys.

descriptions: Lucchese family cooperated with Tampa family in narcotics trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1950s, Lucchese controlled a narcotic trafficking network with Santo Trafficante Jr., the boss of the Tampa crime family.

descriptions: Lucchese and Trafficante Jr. cooperated in narcotics trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: The crew was controlled by Caporegime Paul Vario during the early 1960s into the early 1980s

descriptions: The Bensonhurst Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The 19th Hole Crew , sometimes called Bensonhurst Crew , was based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn , before it was merged into the Brooklyn faction.

edges.evidence_quote: A "five star general", or high ranking member, of the Nine Trey Gangsters with ties to the Lucchese crime family was arrested in December 2023

edges.evidence_quote: Jackie DiNorscio ... later defects to the Lucchese crime family due to a bloody internal feud within the Philadelphia crime family.

descriptions: The Robert's Lounge Crew was associated with the Lucchese family through Burke's friendship with Vario.
edges.evidence_quote: Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.

descriptions: Rudaj Organization attacked Lucchese associates over gambling rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Rudaj led an attack in August 2001 on two Greek associates of the Lucchese crime family who ran a gambling racket inside a Greek social club on 30th St. in Queens.

descriptions: Tanglewood Boys served as a recruitment gang for the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tanglewood Boys was an Italian-American recruitment gang or " farm team " for the American Mafia , specifically the Lucchese crime family .

descriptions: The Velentzas Organization had a cooperative relationship with the Lucchese crime family, receiving protection and sharing profits.
edges.evidence_quote: Velentzas maintained a close working relationship with Lucchese crime family's Consigliere Christopher 'Christie Tick' Furnari. The Lucchese family provided protection to the Greeks, and received a portion of their gambling profits.

San Francisco crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in San Francisco, California, organized in the early 1930s by Francesco 'Frank' Lanza. The family was involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, narcotics trafficking, and other illegal activities. Its membership peaked at 15-20 made men under boss James 'Jimmy the Hat' Lanza. The family was active from around 1928 until 2006.
